title: Tangy Silky Light Argentinian Redcurrants Tart
description: A light and tangy tart featuring Argentinian redcurrants, perfect for a weeknight dessert. This recipe is Jain-friendly and can be made in under an hour.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and roll out the dough to a thickness of 3mm.
Blind bake the tart crust for 15 minutes, then set aside to cool.
In a saucepan, combine the redcurrants, sugar, and almond milk and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the berries burst.
Add the red wine vinegar to the saucepan and deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Roll out the remaining dough to a thickness of 3mm and use a pastry cutter or a knife to cut out long, thin strips for the lattice top.
Place the lattice strips on top of the tart and press the edges to seal.
Brush the lattice top with the beaten egg and sprinkle with sugar.
Bake the tart for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbly.
Allow the tart to cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.
